original_image_tags: Aged, light-yellow paper page with two hole-punch marks at the top. The page contains faded, backward-bleeding text from the reverse side and is largely blank except for stamps in the lower right quadrant.
A stamped block in red ink, oriented upside down relative to the main page view.
A blue ink stamp, oriented upside down relative to the main page view, and a small handwritten notation.

elements.description: One red rectangular stamp in the bottom right corner. The text inside reads: 'U.S. DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION', 'COMMUNICATIONS SECTION', 'MAIL ROOM', 'RECEIVED-MAIL ROOM', and a date and time stamp 'JUL 1 2 1947 P.M. 4:44 P.M. #17'. The paper is aged and yellowed, with two hole punches at the top. There is faint, mirrored text visible through the paper.

judge.reason: Both sides agree on the page having two hole-punches, aged yellowed paper, faint mirrored text, and a red rectangular stamp in the bottom right, but Description B adds specific quoted text from the stamp that Description A omits without contradiction.
